session.user:
Update all references to old class names in .claude/skills/ directory. Replace: - `PlanBackend` → `ManagedPrBackend` - `PlannedPRBackend` → `GitHubManagedPrBackend` - `FakePlanBackend` → `FakeManagedPrBackend` - `GitHubPlanBackend` → `GitHubManagedPrBackend` (the current name of the implementation class)
Files to update (found via grep): 1. .claude/skills/fake-driven-testing/references/anti-patterns.md 2. .claude/skills/fake-driven-testing/references/gateway-architecture.md
These are skill reference documents. Read each file, make the replacements, and write back.
Important: - The provider name "github-draft-pr" should NOT be changed (serialization name) - When updating code examples, also update method names: `.create_plan(` → `.create_managed_pr(`, etc. - Use the Edit tool with replace_all where appropriate for each file
session.assistant: I'll update the references to the old class names in the skill files. Let me first read both files to see what needs to be changed.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: fake-driven-testing-gateway-architecture 3→description: Gateway layer architecture and interface patterns 4→--- 5→ 6→# Gateway Layer Architecture 7→ 8→**Read this when**: You need to understand or modify the gateway layer (the thin wrapper interfaces over external state). 9→ 10→## Overview 11→ 12→**Naming note**: "Gateway" is a common name for this pattern. These classes are also called **adapters**, **providers**, or **ports** in other contexts. The pattern matters more than the name. 13→ 14→## What Are Gateway Classes? 15→ 16→**Gateway classes are thin wrappers around heavyweight external APIs** that: 17→ 18→- Touch external state (filesystem, database, APIs, message queues) 19→- Could be slow (network calls, disk I/O, subprocess execution) 20→- Could fail periodically (network issues, rate limits, service outages) 21→- Are difficult to test directly 22→ 23→## The Four Implementations 24→ 25→Every gateway interface has **four implementations**: 26→ 27→### 1. Abstract Interface (ABC) 28→ 29→Defines the contract all implementations must follow. 30→
... [543 lines omitted] ...
113→ except Exception: 125→- Lets exceptions bubble up (no try/except for control flow) 158→ # Simulate failure if configured 160→ raise RuntimeError(f"Simulated failure for: {sql}") 344→ raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}") 363→ raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}") 494→ raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}") 497→**Exception**: Fakes may create real directories when necessary for integration, but should not read/write actual files.

1→--- 2→name: fake-driven-testing-anti-patterns 3→description: Common testing mistakes to avoid 4→--- 5→ 6→# Testing Anti-Patterns 7→ 8→**Read this when**: You're unsure if your approach is correct, or want to avoid common mistakes. 9→ 10→## Overview 11→ 12→This document covers common anti-patterns in Python testing and how to avoid them. Each anti-pattern includes examples of what NOT to do and the correct approach. 13→ 14→## ❌ Testing Speculative Features 15→ 16→**NEVER write tests for code that doesn't exist yet** (unless doing TDD RIGHT NOW). 17→ 18→### Wrong Approach 19→ 20→```python 21→# ❌ WRONG: Placeholder test for future feature 22→# def test_feature_we_might_add_later(): 23→# """TODO: Implement this feature next sprint.""" 24→# pass 25→ 26→# ❌ WRONG: Test stub for "maybe someday" idea 27→# def test_hypothetical_feature(): 28→# """Feature we're considering for Q2.""" 29→# # Not implemented yet, just a placeholder 30→# pass
... [809 lines omitted] ...
194→4. **CI/CD failures**: Paths may not exist on CI systems 195→5. **Permission errors**: Tests may not have write access to hardcoded paths 225→ # Old signature - type error! 231→ # Old signature - type error! 234→# Result: Type errors, broken tests, runtime failures 256→- **Type errors**: Implementations don't match interface 257→- **Runtime errors**: Tests pass locally but fail in production 295→# ✅ CORRECT: Fast, better error messages (for Click CLIs) 329→- subprocess: Harder to set breakpoints, unclear errors 330→- Test clients: Direct access to exceptions, clear stack traces 525→### Exception: Directory Creation 604→ with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Invalid order"): 819→| Not updating all layers | Type errors, broken tests | Update ABC/Real/Fake/DryRun |
session.assistant: Now I'll update both files with the renamed classes. I'll use the Edit tool with replace_all where appropriate.

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: - **Backends** = higher-level abstractions that COMPOSE gateways (PlannedPRBackend) - Only need ABC + real implementations - **NO fake implementation needed** - inject fake gateways instead
- **Backends** = higher-level abstractions that COMPOSE gateways (GitHubManagedPrBackend) - Only need ABC + real implementations - **NO fake implementation needed** - inject fake gateways instead

tool_use.name: Edit
Edit
Edit
tool_use.param: ``` CLI → ErkContext (DI container) → GitHubPlanBackend (backend - REAL in tests) → FakeGitHubIssues (gateway - FAKE in tests) ← DI stops here ```
``` CLI → ErkContext (DI container) → GitHubManagedPrBackend (backend - REAL in tests) → FakeGitHubIssues (gateway - FAKE in tests) ← DI stops here ```

tool_use.name: Edit
Edit
tool_use.param: ```python # ✅ CORRECT: Inject fake gateways into real backend def test_create_plan(): ...[truncated 247 chars]...: Creating a fake backend class FakePlanBackend(PlanBackend): # DON'T DO THIS ... ```

```python # ✅ CORRECT: Inject fake gateways into real backend def test_create_managed_pr()...[truncated 287 chars]... a fake backend class FakeManagedPrBackend(ManagedPrBackend): # DON'T DO THIS ... ```

session.assistant:
Perfect! I've successfully updated all references to the old class names in both skill reference documents. Here's a summary of the changes made:
## Updated Files
### 1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/fake-driven-testing/references/anti-patterns.md`
**Class name updates:** - `PlanBackend` → `ManagedPrBackend` - `GitHubPlanBackend` → `GitHubManagedPrBackend` - `FakePlanBackend` → `FakeManagedPrBackend` - `PlannedPRBackend` → `GitHubManagedPrBackend` (in DI container example)
**Method name updates:** - `.create_plan(...)` → `.create_managed_pr(...)` - `CreatePlanResult` → `CreateManagedPrResult` - `test_create_plan()` → `test_create_managed_pr()`
**Variable name updates:** - `plans` → `managed_prs` (in fake backend constructor)
### 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/fake-driven-testing/references/gateway-architecture.md`
**Class name updates:** - `PlannedPRBackend` → `GitHubManagedPrBackend` (in table and examples) - `PlanBackend` → `ManagedPrBackend` - `FakePlanBackend` → `FakeManagedPrBackend`
**Method name updates:** - `.create_plan(...)` → `.create_managed_pr(...)` - `CreatePlanResult` → `CreateManagedPrResult` - `test_create_plan()` → `test_create_managed_pr()`
**Variable name updates:** - `plan_backend` → `managed_pr_backend`
All changes have been completed successfully. The skill reference documents now use the correct terminology consistent with the semantic rename from "plan" to "managed PR" for the backend architecture.
